concepts.title: Mendel's Laws of Inheritance
concepts.description: Mendel's laws of inheritance include the Law of Segregation and the Law of Independent Assortment. The Law of Segregation states that each pair of alleles separates during gamete formation, while the Law of Independent Assortment states that alleles of different genes are sorted independently of each other during gamete formation.

concepts.title: Types of Inheritance
concepts.description: There are two main types of inheritance - Mendelian Inheritance and Non-Mendelian Inheritance. Mendelian Inheritance involves the inheritance of traits in a predictable pattern, while Non-Mendelian Inheritance involves the inheritance of traits in a non-predictable pattern, such as incomplete dominance and polygenic inheritance.

concepts.title: Genotype and Phenotype
concepts.description: Genotype refers to the genetic makeup of an organism, while phenotype refers to the physical expression of the genotype. An organism's genotype determines its phenotype.

concepts.title: Polygenic Inheritance
concepts.description: Polygenic inheritance involves the inheritance of multiple genes that contribute to a single trait. This type of inheritance is often seen in complex traits, such as height and skin color.

concepts.title: Mendel's Law of Segregation
concepts.description: Students often confuse the Law of Segregation with the Law of Independent Assortment. The Law of Segregation states that each pair of alleles separates during gamete formation, while the Law of Independent Assortment states that alleles of different genes are sorted independently of each other during gamete formation. To avoid this mistake, students should carefully read and understand the definitions of each law.

concepts.title: AABB and AaBb Genotypes
concepts.description: To remember the difference between AABB and AaBb genotypes, use the mnemonic 'AABB is like a pair of shoes, while AaBb is like a mix-and-match puzzle'. AABB represents a genotype where both alleles are the same, while AaBb represents a genotype where both alleles are different.

concepts.title: Incomplete Dominance
concepts.description: Incomplete dominance is a type of non-Mendelian inheritance where one allele does not completely dominate the other allele, resulting in a blending of the two alleles.

concepts.title: Punnett Square
concepts.description: A Punnett square is a diagram used to predict the probability of different genotypes and phenotypes in offspring. It is a useful tool for understanding Mendelian inheritance and predicting the outcomes of genetic crosses.
